articles.title: Bengaluru street vendors fighting to stay in business amid LPG crisis
articles.description: Street vendors: Bengaluru’s food stalls face losses amid LPG shortage, rising cylinder prices and black market costs, forcing menu cuts, price hikes and closures, while customers see reduced quality and fewer options across key areas.

articles.title: NFHS-6 data likely to be out in May
articles.description: For the current round, conducted digitally, 6,79,238 households were surveyed across states and UTs between 2023 and 2024. The 6th round may provide details about Covid-19 cases, deaths and hospitalisation.

articles.title: Why gardeners are using orange peels to grow healthier plants naturally
articles.description: Orange peels offer a sustainable solution for gardeners. They can be composted to enrich soil with essential nutrients. A natural insect repellent can be made from dried peels. Orange peels also serve as biodegradable seed starters. A spray made from peels effectively combats aphids. These simple uses reduce household waste and promote healthier plants naturally.

articles.title: FIFA removes Toronto World Cup resale tickets after new price cap legislation
articles.description: April 28 : FIFA has removed World Cup tickets for matches at Toronto's BMO Field stadium from their official resale marketplace after the Canadian province of Ontario passed legislation last week banning the reselling of event tickets above face value.The bill, called the 'Putting Fans First Act', says no per

articles.title: Downside growth risks outweigh inflation concerns, RBI likely to hold rates this fiscal: Crisil
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee is expected to hold interest rates steady this fiscal year. Economic growth concerns are seen as more pressing than inflation risks. Producers face higher costs, but consumer prices are contained. The government aims to reduce its fiscal deficit. Market borrowing will increase. The current account deficit is projected to widen.

articles.description: April 28 : NXP Semiconductors forecast second-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street expectations on Tuesday, betting on an ongoing recovery in the industrial and automotive chip markets to boost revenue, sending its shares up 15 per cent in extended trading.NXP derives most of its revenue from the auto

articles.title: Oil Price Today (April 29): Crude oil crosses $110, extends gain for 8th straight session. Here’s why
articles.description: Oil prices continued their rally for an eighth consecutive day, driven by reports of a potential extended U.S. blockade of Iranian ports. This move, aimed at pressuring Iran's economy and oil exports, could prolong supply disruptions from the Middle East. The Strait of Hormuz's reduced accessibility has already significantly impacted energy supplies, leading to fears of a fresh inflation crisis.

articles.title: India fast-tracks Canada FTA, GCC talks paused, says Piyush Goyal
articles.description: India is pushing forward with trade deals. Negotiations for a free trade agreement with Canada are speeding up. Talks are also underway with Chile and Peru. However, discussions with the Gulf Cooperation Council have been temporarily halted due to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. India plans to waive intellectual property fees for sporting goods for three years.

articles.title: 'My 40-day salary slip': Bank of Baroda PO reveals first payslip, with allowances & perks fueling viral buzz
articles.description: A Bank of Baroda probationary officer’s viral payslip has sparked fresh interest in bank PO salaries and government banking jobs in India. The officer revealed an in-hand amount of Rs 98,745 for 40 days, along with a wide range of perks such as accommodation, allowances, and medical benefits. The post has triggered discussions around the financial stability, benefits, and long-term security offered by public sector bank jobs, especially as similar salary reveals from SBI officers continue to gain attention online.

articles.title: Saudi Aramco halts LPG exports through May after damage
articles.description: Saudi Aramco will halt liquefied petroleum gas shipments next month. The Juaymah export facility sustained damage in February. Repairs are delayed, preventing shipments through May. This disruption impacts buyers, especially in Asia. India, reliant on LPG for cooking, faces significant effects. The regional conflict further complicates energy supplies.

articles.title: MCM set to hire agency for fresh waste processing
articles.description: MCM to hire private agency for 3-month fresh waste processing in Manesar at Rs 85 lakh; city generates 230–250 tonnes daily; weighbridge from May 1.

articles.title: India industrial output at 4.1%, hit by Iran war
articles.description: India's industrial output grew 4.1% in March, a five-month low. This slowdown was influenced by weaker electricity and manufacturing sectors. Economists noted the impact of the Iran conflict on supplies and costs. Despite the moderation, growth surpassed expectations. Manufacturing saw a dip, though capital goods showed strong investment demand. Government spending supported infrastructure growth.

articles.title: What is a cooling vest, and why are over 2,500 Indian food delivery agents trying it amidst summer heat
articles.description: Zomato is piloting cooling vests for over 2,500 delivery partners in 14 cities facing extreme heat. These PVA-based vests offer up to 4 hours of evaporative cooling, designed for temperatures exceeding 40°C. Developed through a collaboration, they aim to reduce physical stress and prevent overheating for workers on the front lines.

articles.title: India was 5th biggest military spender in 2025, expenditure rose by 8.9% to $92 bn; China’s spend $336bn; Pak $11.9bn: Report
articles.description: India ranked as the fifth largest military spender globally in 2025, with an 8.9% increase to $92.1 billion, influenced by the India-Pakistan conflict. Global military expenditure reached $2,887 billion, an 11th consecutive year of growth, driven by Europe and Asia, despite a US spending decrease.

articles.title: This man was laid off from Amazon at 43. Here's how he got a new job in 90 days
articles.description: The professional described how he relied heavily on networking, not just within his immediate circle but beyond it. 'I ended every single catch-up call with one simple ask: ‘Can you forward my CV to two people in your network who should see it?'' he said, explaining that opportunities often exist within extended professional connections.

articles.title: Textiles, jewellery, leather seen leading gains from India–New Zealand FTA; broader export impact may be limited
articles.description: The agreement, signed on April 27, reduces or eliminates tariffs on 100 percent of Indian exports to New Zealand, while India will offer market access on about 70 percent of tariff lines covering roughly 95 percent of New Zealand’s exports, including wool, coal, wood and wine.

articles.title: Tax saving investments in India: 7 things you need to know
articles.description: Most Indians overpay income tax simply because they do not plan ahead. The Income Tax Act offers multiple legal ways to reduce your tax bill — through Section 80C, 80D, 80CCD, and more. A salaried person in the highest tax bracket can save up to Rs 46,800 in taxes on a single Rs 1.5 lakh investment. The only catch? You need to act before the financial year ends. You are paying more tax than you should
